name: code-style-guide
description: Review code against a specific style guide and suggest fixes
usage: Add source files and set the target style guide, then copy the prompt
promptInstructions:
Review all provided code for compliance with the **{{.stateKeys.style}}** style guide.

{{.styleInstructions}}

Target language: {{.stateKeys.language}} (if "auto", infer from file extensions).

For each violation found:
1. Quote the offending line(s)
2. Cite the specific style rule being violated
3. Provide the corrected version
4. Rate severity: error (must fix), warning (should fix), or info (nitpick)

styleInstructions.google: Follow the Google style guide conventions. Prioritize readability, consistency, and the principle of least surprise. Pay special attention to naming conventions, comment quality, and function length.
styleInstructions.airbnb: Follow the Airbnb style guide conventions. Enforce destructuring, arrow functions, template literals, and module import/export patterns. Flag any use of var or loose equality.
styleInstructions.standard: Follow the Standard JS style conventions. No semicolons, 2-space indentation, single quotes. Flag any deviations from the no-configuration rule set.
styleInstructions.pep8: Follow PEP 8 conventions for Python. Enforce 4-space indentation, snake_case naming, line length limits (79 for code, 72 for docstrings), and proper import ordering.
styleInstructions.effective-go: Follow Effective Go and the Go Code Review Comments guide. Enforce gofmt formatting, MixedCaps naming, error handling patterns, and idiomatic Go constructs.

commands.name: note
commands.description: Add a note about specific concerns

commands.name: set-style
commands.description: Set the target style guide (google, airbnb, standard, pep8, effective-go)

commands.name: set-language
commands.description: Set the target language (auto, javascript, python, go, java, etc.)
